name: Passover Chocolate Roll
recipeIngredient: 6 Egg whites - Keep at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease the bottom of a 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 x 1-inch jelly-roll pan; line with waxed paper, greased lightly.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F.
3. In a large bowl of an electric mixer, beat egg whites at high speed until soft peaks form when beaters are slowly raised.
4. Add 1/4 cup sugar, 2 tablespoons at a time, beating until stiff peaks form when beater is slowly raised.
5. With the same beaters, beat yolks at high speed, adding the remaining 1/2 cup of sugar in 2 tablespoon increments, until the mixture is very thick - about 4 minutes.
6. At low speed, beat in cocoa, vanilla, and salt just until smooth.
7. Using a wire whisk or rubber scraper, gently fold the cocoa mixture into the beaten egg whites, just until they are blended (no egg white should show).
8. Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan.
9. Bake for 15 minutes, just until the surface springs back when gently pressed with a fingertip.
10. Sift confectioner's sugar over parchment on a clean linen towel.
11. Turn cake out onto the sugar, lifting off the pan and peeling off the paper from the cake.
12. Roll up the cake jelly-roll fashion, towel and all, starting at the short end.
13. Cool completely on a rack, seam side down - at least 30 minutes.
14. To make the filling: Combine the filling ingredients in a medium bowl. Beat with an electric mixer until thick and then refrigerate.
15. Unroll the cake, spread with filling to 1-inch from the edge, and reroll. Refrigerate for 1 hour before serving.
16. To serve, sprinkle with confectioner's sugar. You may make this cake a week ahead; then freeze it wrapped in foil. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour before serving.
Note: Pure vanilla has alcohol and is not Kosher for Passover. Either leave it out or find vanilla without the alcohol. Confectioner's sugar has cornstarch and is not Kosher for Passover. Someone posted a recipe for making confectioner's sugar using potato starch instead of cornstarch. I did not get the exact directions, but you may find it in one of the dessert recipes.
